original_abstract: Internalizing religious values through a structured "Faithful Friday" (Jum’at Beriman) culture serves as a strategic intervention for enhancing student character. This study investigates the specific religious values embedded in this weekly tradition and their impact on character strengthening at Jalaluddin Vocational School (SMK), Wonosobo. Utilizing a qualitative field research design with a case study approach, data were gathered through observations, interviews, and documentation involving school leadership, teachers, and students. The findings reveal that the Faithful Friday program incorporates activities such as Quranic recitation, congregational Dhuha prayers, Tahlil, and Mujahadah. These practices successfully internalize core religious values, specifically values of faith (aqidah), worship (ibadah), and wisdom (kearifan). The systematic integration of these values fosters essential character traits in students, including religious devotion, social awareness, environmental care, peacefulness, responsibility, and leadership skills. This school culture effectively bridges the gap between theoretical religious education and practical daily behavior, offering a model for holistic character development in vocational education settings.
humanized_abstract: Abstract: This study investigated the religious values embodied by students through a Culture of Faithful Friday (Jum’at Beriman) and its influence towards strengthening the students’ character. Based on the concept of the Culture of Faithful Friday (Jum’at Beriman), the educational values of religion which are enlivened through Friday atmosphere should be known and mastered by students, so that it is able to support an educational process aimed at fostering students’ character in SMK. The research design employed in this study is qualitative field research using the case study method by collecting data from school leaders, teachers, and students. The results of this study showed that activities enlivened in SMK are reading the Quran and performing congregational prayer in the morning (Dhuha) and Tahlil and Mujahadah. In line with the philosophy of culture of Faithful Friday, these religious activities aim at disseminating religious values relating to aqidah (faith), ibadah (worship), and kearifan (wisdom). As a result, a number of characters such as students’ faithfulness, awareness towards social, environmental, peace, peace, accountability, and leadership are built and strengthening students’ character, therefore bridging the normative values of religion in schools into students’ actions both in and out of class. Hence, SMK can become a role model in terms of nurturing character through vocational education activities at schools.
